title: Temporomandibular joint disorders
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Temporomandibular joint disorders can cause jaw pain, clicking, or pain with chewing.
Supportive care may include avoiding hard chewing, jaw rest, and clinician-directed pain control.
Trauma, dental infection, deep-space infection, trismus, neurologic symptoms, or chest-pain mimics require separate evaluation.
